component.name: Swatchbook
component.summary: Catálogo de estilos basado en Markdown

description.p: Swatchbook is a Markdown-powered design-token binder for GNOME. Write colour palettes, typography scales, spacing systems, border radii, and shadow definitions in plain .md files and watch them render as an interactive canvas in real time.
Every inline-code span in a list item becomes a live token card. Click any card to copy its value to the clipboard.

ul.li: Colours: #rrggbb, #rgba, rgb(), rgba(), hsl(), all 148 CSS named colours, and transparent
Typography: font: Family Bold 16px/1.5 — renders a live "Ag" type specimen via Pango
Spacing: bare size values like 8px or 0.5rem — rendered as a measured bar
Radius: radius: 6px — rendered as a rounded box preview
Shadows: shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.12) — rendered as a layered card preview
WCAG contrast checking built in — AA / AAA levels computed from colour tokens
Export to PNG, SVG, W3C JSON design tokens, GIMP palette, CSS custom properties, and Tailwind config
Click to copy any token value; keyboard arrow-key navigation on the canvas
Syntax-highlighted Markdown editor with live 150 ms preview debounce
Auto-save every 30 s with crash recovery
Dark mode, responsive split-view layout, multi-window support
Registered with the system's recent-files list on open and save

description.p: Swatchbook te permite escribir paletas de colores, tipografías, espaciados, radios de borde y sombras en Markdown simple, y los renderiza como fichas interactivas dentro de una ventana nativa de GNOME.

screenshot.caption: Live swatch preview alongside the Markdown editor

description.p: Full design-token support: Font, Spacing, Radius, and Shadow cards join Colour swatches. Export to W3C JSON design tokens, GIMP palette, and Tailwind config. Live syntax highlighting in the editor. Recent-files integration. WCAG contrast checking. Full CSS colour support (148 named colours, rgba, hsl, transparent, alpha hex).
Live language switching, restricted Flatpak permissions, Flathub screenshots

First stable release of Swatchbook — a Markdown-powered colour-swatch editor for GNOME. Write your palette in Markdown and watch the live canvas update in real time. Features: live swatch preview with dark-mode support, keyboard navigation (arrow keys + Enter to copy hex), PNG and SVG export, CSS custom-property export, file open/save with auto-save and crash recovery, multi-window support, responsive split-view layout, and a built-in language switcher with translations in Spanish, French and German.
Fix preview and about me section
Fix canvas not rendering swatches; fix About dialog now floats freely; correct version shown in About; release script now keeps metainfo in sync.
Update CI actions to natively target Node.js 24, silencing the Node.js 20 deprecation warning.
Fix duplicate Spanish entries in desktop and metainfo files that broke the release build.
Scrollable canvas, crash-recovery fix, CSS slug deduplication, modified-flag indicator, 16 new tests.
All phases completed: live Markdown parsing, canvas renderer, file I/O, auto-save, Spanish translation, and release automation.
Initial release of the Markdown style binder.
